page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the N1 machine family. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 1 physical core of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is configured with 13.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 6.5 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the n1-highmem-2 occupies the lower tiers of compute and memory throughput. The single-core stress-ng benchmark score of 1105.58 and Geekbench single-core score of 944.0 place it in the bottom 25% of tested servers. Multi-core performance is further constrained, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 1124.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 1452.88, both falling into the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is similarly limited, registering 13.32 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 11.13 GB/sec for write operations.

Given its hardware profile, the instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require a larger memory footprint than standard general-purpose instances but do not demand high CPU throughput. It is suitable for small-scale databases, caching layers like Redis (which achieved 1.38 million SET operations per second in testing), lightweight static web serving, and basic memory-bound background tasks. It is not recommended for compute-intensive applications, large language model inference, or high-throughput data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 13.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.3 GHz, it allocates 6.5 GB of memory per vCPU. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark evaluations place its performance in the lower percentiles, with a single-core Geekbench score of 944.0 and a multi-core score of 1124.0. Large-block memory read bandwidth is measured at 13.32 GB/sec. Due to these performance characteristics, the n1-highmem-2 is best suited for memory-sensitive but compute-light workloads, such as small database hosting, caching layers, and basic web serving, offering a qualitative balance of memory capacity for entry-level workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-2 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 13.0 GB of RAM, designed for memory-skewed, low-compute workloads like caching and small databases.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-2 is an x86_64 virtual machine with 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 13.0 GB of RAM for memory-skewed workloads.
tagline: An entry-level, memory-skewed virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors for lightweight caching and database workloads.
bullet_points: Configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 13.0 GB of system memory
Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for small databases, caching layers, and static web serving
Delivers 1.38 million Redis SET operations per second
